note: SNG-082 — TERRAIN data so the world map can render actual terrestrial features instead of nodes on a dark panel (Erik 2026-07-12: 'especially if the background was an actual map with terrestrial features'). Data-driven, NOT an authored image: a region's terrain, elevation, palette, features and water are DERIVED from its dispositional identity, so a BATCH-9-generated location automatically inherits the right terrain. Authored by Aevi.

renderGuidance.regionShape: Compute a hull/voronoi from each region's locations' map.x/y and fill it with the region's palette. Region boundaries need not be crisp — dispositional space has soft edges.
renderGuidance.water: Draw each region's water features. The Echo River (valley) and THE SEA (the_feeling_coast) are the map's two great water landmarks.
renderGuidance.roads: Location `connections` ARE the roads. The Axis Gate's twelve roads are the map's spine — draw them heaviest.
renderGuidance.elevation: Use it for hatching/relief. The Ascent is the highest point; the Umbral Depths are BELOW the map.
renderGuidance.honestFailures: Three regions must LOOK wrong, deliberately: the_pattern_reach (resists mapping), the_veiled_reach (the map is lying), the_numinous_reach (the cartography loses confidence). Do not clean these up — the failure is the content.
renderGuidance.growth: The Blaze, the Churn Edge, the Scouring and the Ceaseless are all EXPANDING. If cheap, show it.

regions.name: The Valley of Echoes
regions.elevation: lowland, rising to foothills N and E
water: Echo River (runs W→E through the basin)
regions.visualIdentity: Green, worked, human. The only place on the map that looks LIVED IN rather than believed in. The centre of the map's warmth.

regions.name: The Crossing
regions.terrain: a great crossroads plain, ringed by the twelve roads
features: THE GREAT COLISEUM (the largest structure on the map)
the AXIS GATE — twelve roads radiating outward, one per axis
water: cisterns and canals — the water comes from everywhere
regions.visualIdentity: The hub. Every road on the map ends here. Draw the twelve roads as the map's spine — the Axis Gate is the visual centre of the world.

regions.name: The Umbral Depths
regions.terrain: subterranean — a vast cave-city system beneath the northern reaches
regions.elevation: BELOW sea level; render as a cutaway or a shaded under-region
features: the Slow Stair (the only surface entrance — draw it)
regions.visualIdentity: Ink-dark. It should read as UNDER the map, not on it — a hole in the world with one stair leading down.

regions.name: The Radiant Wastes
regions.terrain: glass plain — land fused to glass by the Blaze
features: the great focusing arrays of the Lensward
THE BLAZE — an expanding white scar, and it should look like it is GROWING
regions.visualIdentity: Blinding pale. The only bright region on the map. Its edge should be visibly EATING the land around it.

regions.name: The Quickwood
features: the Greenward (a living wall — draw it thorned)
regions.visualIdentity: The deepest green on the map. Its cities are indistinguishable from its forest — that IS the point.

regions.name: The Palelands
features: the Hollowing (the border with the Quickwood — the sharpest visual seam on the map)
regions.visualIdentity: Grey and TENDED — not a wasteland; a garden of endings. Its border with the Quickwood is life meeting death, and should look it.

regions.name: The Riven Marches
features: the Redline (a scar across the land)
THE STILLHOLD — white walls, and they should look like a lid on something
regions.visualIdentity: Iron and old blood. The Stillhold's white walls sit inside it like a held breath.

regions.name: The Somatic Reaches
features: THE COGITARIUM (a single vast enclosed structure — the antipodal contrast)
regions.visualIdentity: Body and mind as ARCHITECTURE: the Temples are open to the sky, the Cogitarium is sealed. Draw the contrast.

regions.name: The Unspooling / The Churn
regions.terrain: unstable terrain — it is not the same twice
features: the Churn Edge (a creeping, ragged border)
regions.visualIdentity: The only region that should look UNCERTAIN — soft edges, doubled lines, a border that is visibly creeping outward.

regions.name: The Deepwood
features: the Blocklands (precursor geometry breaking the surface)
regions.visualIdentity: Older than everything. Precursor geometry showing through the trees like bone through skin.

regions.name: The Lattice-Cities
features: stacked arcologies (draw them as a GRID — perfect, and slightly horrible)
the Grand Lattice at the centre, brightest and most regular
regions.visualIdentity: The most REGULAR thing on the map. Every line straight. It should be beautiful and make you uneasy.

regions.name: The Gearlands
features: the Service Ways (dashed lines UNDER everything — they run beneath other regions)
THE GREAT ENGINE — mountain-sized, and it should dominate its horizon
regions.visualIdentity: Brass and pressure. The Service Ways should be drawn running under the whole map — the Gearlands touch everywhere from below.

regions.name: The Numinous Reach
features: the Numen (draw it FADING — the map itself should lose confidence here)
regions.visualIdentity: The one region whose EDGES SHOULD BE UNCERTAIN on the map. The cartography fails here, and that is honest.

regions.name: The Feeling Coast
water: THE SEA — the map's only ocean. It should be big.
regions.visualIdentity: Tidal. The only coastline in the world. Everything here is shaped by something arriving and leaving.

regions.name: The Reasoned Hold
features: the Axiom (a city built like an argument — draw it as derivation)
regions.visualIdentity: Cold, clear, and rational. Its geometry is DERIVED, not imposed — subtly different from the Lattice.

regions.name: The Mirrorlands
regions.terrain: mirrored terrain — it does not survey correctly
regions.visualIdentity: The map is LYING here and should look like it — doubled features, an outline that doesn't quite close.
regions.renameNote: The map lies there; mirrors are its whole idiom. (SNG-166 §2 / Erik 2026-07-19. Ids are unchanged — saves, regionsKnown and substrate keys all key on the id.)

regions.name: The Told Ground
features: THE TOLD GROUND — a field of standing stones, each one a sentence
water: none, and they will tell you so
regions.visualIdentity: Utterly bare. No decoration anywhere. The Told Ground's stones are the only marks, and there are thousands.
regions.renameNote: Its great feature is a field of standing stones, each one a sentence. The name was already in its own canon. (SNG-166 §2 / Erik 2026-07-19. Ids are unchanged — saves, regionsKnown and substrate keys all key on the id.)

regions.name: The Descent
features: THE MAW — a hole, and it should be the darkest point on the map
regions.visualIdentity: Warm, red, and going DOWN. Handsome, welcoming architecture — that is the trap of it.

regions.name: The Ascent
regions.elevation: the highest place on the map
features: THE UNFALLEN — burning white at the summit
regions.visualIdentity: The highest point. Luminous, tiered, and bearing DOWN on everything below it.

regions.name: The Given Land
features: Bedrock (a city that will outlast the argument about it)
regions.visualIdentity: The most SOLID region on the map. Everything drawn heavy. Nothing decorative.

regions.name: Cloudform
regions.visualIdentity: The cartographer gave up. Draw it as a region that will not hold its own shape — the honest failure of a map.
regions.renameNote: Its city is Cloudform and its terrain will not hold a shape. (SNG-166 §2 / Erik 2026-07-19. Ids are unchanged — saves, regionsKnown and substrate keys all key on the id.)

regions.name: The Long Horizon
features: the Long Span (a MOVING settlement — mark it as provisional)
the Wayhouse (the only fixed thing, and they are embarrassed by it)
regions.visualIdentity: Roads and horizon. The settlement should be marked as MOVING — it will not be there next season.
regions.renameNote: Endless roads and a settlement that MOVES. Horizon is already its word. (SNG-166 §2 / Erik 2026-07-19. Ids are unchanged — saves, regionsKnown and substrate keys all key on the id.)

regions.name: The Kept Hours
features: THE STOPPED — draw it as a place where the map's own time has halted
regions.visualIdentity: Every structure is a clock. The Stopped should look like a still frame inside a moving picture.
regions.renameNote: Clock-country: archives, the Slow Hour. The name should say TIME, not PLACE. (SNG-166 §2 / Erik 2026-07-19. Ids are unchanged — saves, regionsKnown and substrate keys all key on the id.)

regions.name: The Unmade
regions.visualIdentity: Ruins that were ENDED WELL — clean lines, nothing rotting. The Scouring's edge is bare and moving.

regions.name: The Making
features: THE HALF-CATHEDRAL (unfinished, glorious, and the map should show the scaffolding)
regions.visualIdentity: Nothing finished. Scaffolding everywhere. It should look JOYOUS and slightly out of control.
